title: If Evas are alive why do they plug in
display_title: If Evas are alive why do they plug in
wikitext:
[]: If Evas are alive, why do they plug in?             Quote Post by Shinji » Wed Nov 24, 2010 3:17 pm Ok I understand that they're man made and all that. But aren't Evas supposed to be living things? If they are, then why is it that they need batteries and a cable to keep them energized? Is it powering some kind of exoskeleton that does all the movement? It doesn't make sense to me. Not so much that they need power just that when they're out of power BOOM! that's it, they're dead.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Nov 24, 2010 3:20 pm Do you ever see Eva units at local McDonalds? Thought so. They need to get their daily energy somehow

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Nov 24, 2010 3:23 pm Xard wrote: They need to get their daily energy somehow

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Wed Nov 24, 2010 3:26 pm Azathoth wrote: This. Evas don't normally undergo metabolic processes - when Unit 01 goes berserk in episode 19, her heart starts beating to power her.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Wed Nov 24, 2010 3:32 pm Eva's are almost perfect clones of Adam. With the exception that of an s2organ, an organ that all angels have that converts surrounding energies into such sunlight into energy that can sustain them. Which it would seem Nerv were unable to replicate or simply did not want to give an Eva limitless power. So with the removal of the s2organ, Eva is dependant on an outside power source

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Nov 24, 2010 3:33 pm Some threads about this that you might be interested in: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=2473 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=5332

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Nov 24, 2010 3:36 pm Shinji wrote: So... most of the time it's like they're on a huge life support system or something?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Wed Nov 24, 2010 3:53 pm Azathoth wrote: It's less life support and more just providing raw energy for the Eva to use, as a substitute for the energy normally produced in cellular respiration.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Nov 24, 2010 4:28 pm Shinji wrote: I kinda get what you're saying. I just don't get how electricity is converted into energy for a living being to use.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Wed Nov 24, 2010 4:35 pm Azathoth wrote: The whole umbilical cable thing is far more concerned with dramatic usage and another reinforcement of "hint hint, Evas = mothers" than it is with making any sense at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Nov 24, 2010 4:50 pm Shinji wrote: In this case wouldn't Nerv be the mother and the Evas be the huge babies?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Wed Nov 24, 2010 5:11 pm Yea, they really punch that idea into your head over and over, that basically everything came from Lilith except for like, animals. That and LCL being the basic and only ingredient for Angels and humans and everything so who's to say it can't be used as a power source too? Although, it'd have to be converted into electricity or something to fit with the plug thing but like you said, not everything is meant to be taken literally. Thanks for the feedback guys, I think I have a little more understanding now haha

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Nov 24, 2010 5:19 pm Shinji wrote: Yea, they really punch that idea into your head over and over, that basically everything came from Lilith except for like, animals.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Wed Nov 24, 2010 5:28 pm Azathoth wrote: Animals too. All life on earth, right down to the microorganisms.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Nov 24, 2010 5:35 pm Shinji wrote: Really? I always thought that the animals were just naturally there via evolution, since Humanity is actually the Lilim. Or is it just like, they put the "seeds" of life on earth and humanity evolved to the top of the food chain so they're considered the primary result? And animals are their food?

[]:             Quote Post by Animejunkie » Wed Nov 24, 2010 5:38 pm If im not mistaken theres a point, or multiple, where the cable is referred to as the umbilical cable... just imagine its like a baby recieving nutrients from a mother

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Wed Nov 24, 2010 5:46 pm Azathoth wrote: In Rebuild, Angels explode into a dark red fluid which has yet to be identified - although I suspect myself that it's the Adam-based equivalent of LCL. In NGE, however, they don't - when they explode, they usually leave no trace behind. But since LCL comes solely from Lilith, the Angels are not made of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Podferio » Thu Nov 25, 2010 6:58 am For me this is how it goes: Eva in cage= rest / gets fed with nutrients through cables(?) / some other life support thing that we aren't shown. Eva frozen= preservation (if an eva is not in use then preserving it would cost less than sustaining it) Eva with power cable= note that the Eva is covered with lots of "restraints". basically it's how "man" is suppose to control such a huge living organism. man haven't had any source of power but electricity so it is what was employed to control these restraints. note that an Eva goes berserk only when it lost its power because "no power means no restraints". power isn't necessary for eva to move because it's a living organism, but power is required to control it and make it move the way man wants it to.

[]:             Quote Post by Nightweaver20xx » Thu Nov 25, 2010 11:17 am I always thought it was pretty simple: Evas are restrained/supported using advanced, powered exoskeletons. In order to provide power to those, electrical power is needed, and in great quantities. Unrestrained from the exoskeletons, Evas would collapse to the ground in a heap, UNLESS their soul combines with the pilots at extremely high sync ratios, which renders them "alive" (berserk), and a power source becomes unnecessary.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Thu Nov 25, 2010 11:35 am That presents a good point because it would explain why they need power to move but also why when the power runs out they can't just say "Woo hoo! I'm free!" We can probably assume that the restraints are very heavy, and require a berserk too summon the strength to move with them on. Unit 01 never tears them off until it consumes an S2 engine even though it had gone berserk before that, but that could also be due to Yui's plan of not being discovered by SEELE. When Asuka and Shinji are both in Unit 02 they both concentrate hard enough simultaneously so that it initiates a berserk, but in this case it seems like the Eva isn't berserking at all but rather just doing what it was told, but it seems to summon its own power to achieve this. This is kind of interesting because it seems like when an Eva berserks it does whatever the soul's original would try to do. But that's another thread
